name: KEPIER 14

surface_od_ft.qualifier: c. 98 (pencil marginalia 'c 98 AOD' beside the No.14 Hole heading; printed 'Surface Level ___ O.D.' box left blank in original)

units: fathoms/feet/inches, transcribed verbatim — convert ONLY downstream. NOTE: clerk logged in feet & inches with the Fathoms columns left blank throughout; recorded as [0, feet, ins].

annotations: depth-from-surface column left blank in original for this first row; running depth = thickness = 1'10"

intervals.verbatim: Sand with Decayed Timber & Small Coal. Water.
annotations: decayed timber & small coal are drift debris (not an in-situ coal seam)

intervals.verbatim: Large Sandstone Boulders with Gravel. Coal Traces near Bottom.
annotations: glacial sandstone boulders in a gravel matrix (base of drift), not in-situ bedrock
coal traces near bottom = reworked fragments, not an in-situ seam

intervals.verbatim: Soft Grey Sandy Shale and Soft Shale with Ironstone Nodules. into
annotations: 'into' written in the original = hole bottomed still penetrating this bed at total depth (100'0" = 30.48 m)

notes: Sheet 'SECTION OF BOREHOLES AT KEPIER POWER STATION, DURHAM' for North-Eastern Electric Supply Co. Ltd; communicated by / borer WM. COULSON LTD; One-inch Map 27, Six-inch Map Durham. DATE DISCREPANCY: printed date reads verbatim 'January, 1945', but the workflow index for this hole gives 1939 — 1945 recorded per source. This single page carries TWO holes: 'No.14 Hole' (this record, BGS reg NZ 24 SE 12-14, PAGE NO. 1) and, below it, 'No.15 Hole' (a separate borehole, BGS reg NZ 24 SE 12-15, PAGE NO. 1) which is NOT transcribed here. The clerk logged all figures in feet & inches with the Fathoms columns blank (recorded as [0, ft, in]). Clerk arithmetic is clean throughout: previous depth + thickness = stated depth on every row. Row 1 (Loamy Soil) has a blank depth-from-surface cell in the original; running depth = 1'10". Surface level: printed 'Surface Level ___ O.D.' box left blank; later BGS pencil beside the heading reads 'c 98 AOD' (a matching 'c 98' also pencilled on No.15). Total depth 100'0" = 30.48 m, matching the index depth. Ignored as non-content: BGS blue banner/watermarks/registration stamps, and the top-right filing marks (struck-through 'NZ 24 SE 12', pencil '12' / '38').
